Title: Prosecution closes case against former Perlis MB after 21 witnesses testify
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The prosecution today closed its case in the Sessions Court here after calling 21 witnesses to testify in the trial involving former Perlis Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Azlan Man, who faces 10 charges related to submitting false claims and receiving proceeds from unlawful activities.
Deputy Public Prosecutor Noralis Mat informed Judge Azura Alwi that the prosecution had concluded its case following testimony from the final witness, Ministry of Higher Education Budget Division Chief Senior Assistant Secretary Mawar Awang.
Noralis also told the court that the prosecution would not be offering any witnesses to the defence should the accused be ordered to enter his defence.
Judge Azura then fixed July 30 for both parties to file written submissions, while reply submissions are to be submitted by Sept 7. The court also scheduled Oct 1 for clarification proceedings.
Azlan was represented by lawyers Burhanudeen Abdul Wahid and Ramli Shariff.
The trial, which began on July 18, 2024, heard evidence from several witnesses including former Sri Kedawang Travel & Tour (W) Sdn Bhd (SKTT) managing director Shafiei Ismail, former secretary for both the Perlis State Legislative Assembly and the Perlis State Executive Council Norazlan Yahya, former senior private secretary to Azlan, Herwardy Hashim; and Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) investigating officer Mohamad Safwan Arbangi.
Azlan, 67, is facing five charges of allegedly using documents containing false information to claim overseas holiday benefits for trips to London amounting to more than RM1.18 million, despite the trips allegedly never taking place.
The documents allegedly included invoices and quotations for flights and accommodation issued by SKTT and Aidil Travel & Tours Sdn Bhd, along with Perlis State Government orders used to support transportation and lodging claims.
The alleged offences were said to have been committed at the Perlis State Secretary's Office in Kangar between December 2013 and December 2017.
The charges are framed under Section 18 of the MACC Act 2009, which provides a maximum sentence of 20 years' imprisonment and a fine upon conviction.
The former Bintong Assemblyman also faces five charges of allegedly receiving RM1.06 million in proceeds from unlawful activities involving payments linked to SKTT, Aidil Travel & Tours and Abdul Rahim Halim, who represented Aidil Travel.
Those alleged offences were said to have occurred between Feb 19, 2014 and Dec 2017 at Maybank Jalan Tuanku Abdul Rahman branch, CIMB Bank Putrajaya branch and Sheraton Imperial Hotel here and were framed under the Anti-Money Laundering, Anti-Terrorism Financing and Proceeds of Unlawful Activities Act 2001.
In addition, Azlan also faces alternative charges under Section 403 of the Penal Code involving the alleged misappropriation of Perlis government funds amounting to RM1.06 million for the 2014 to 2017 financial years.
If convicted, Azlan could be sentenced to a prison term of not less than six months and a maximum of five years and caning and a fine. –  BERNAMA
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: International trips lift MAHB's passenger traffic to 7.9mil in May
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia Airports Holdings Bhd (MAHB) recorded 7.9 million passenger movements across its network in May 2026, supported by sustained international travel demand.
In a statement, the airport operator said Kuala Lumpur International Airport (KLIA) handled 4.9 million passengers during the month.
Of KLIA's total passenger traffic, 3.6 million were international travellers, representing a two per cent increase compared to May 2025.
According to MAHB, regional connectivity was further strengthened with the launch of Hainan Airlines' daily direct service between Chongqing and Kuala Lumpur on May 16, enhancing links with China.
MAHB managing director Datuk Mohd Izani Ghani said expanding strategic air connectivity remains important in supporting tourism growth and improving accessibility for international visitors.
"The introduction of Hainan Airlines' direct service between Chongqing and Kuala Lumpur further strengthens our connectivity with China, one of Malaysia's key visitor markets," he said.
During the month, Malaysia Airports also completed several passenger experience enhancements, including upgraded check-in counters at Miri and Limbang airports, an expanded e-hailing pick-up zone at Langkawi International Airport, and the first phase of restroom refurbishment works at Subang Airport.
Meanwhile, Istanbul Sabiha Gökçen International Airport in Türkiye handled 4.1 million passengers in May, up 10.8 per cent from April 2026.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Two hurt in collision involving three-tonne, five-tonne lorries
Article:
NIBONG TEBAL: Two lorry drivers were injured after their vehicles collided along Jalan Taman Kuang Indah in Sungai Jawi near here this morning.
Penang Fire and Rescue Department assistant director of operations John Sagun Francis said firefighters received an emergency call on the incident at 7.04am.
"The accident involved a three-tonne Hino lorry and a five-tonne Hino lorry.
"Both drivers were trapped in their vehicles following the collision," he said today.
John said a team from the Sungai Bakap Fire and Rescue Station arrived at the scene at 7.21am after encountering traffic congestion en route.
He said firefighters carried out rescue operations to extricate the victims from the wreckage.
"The two victims were rescued at 8.21am and given initial treatment by Health Ministry personnel at the scene before being taken by ambulance to Sungai Bakap Hospital," he said.
The 46-year-old driver suffered head injuries and a fractured leg, while the 26-year-old driver sustained head injuries.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: MACC has legal discretion over compounds, says Chief Justice
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Chief Justice Tun Wan Ahmad Farid Wan Salleh said the use of compounds and settlements in corruption-related cases falls within the prerogative of enforcement agencies.
He said enforcement bodies, including the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC), are empowered by law to exercise such discretion.
Wan Ahmad Farid was responding to concerns over the increasing use of compounds and charge withdrawals in corruption-related cases.
"It is beyond me. I cannot comment. I can only comment on matters that come before the court.
"If matters go to court, then we decide one way or the other. Under the law, MACC can do that," he said when met at the Asian International Arbitration Centre headquarters here today.
On June 5, anti-graft watchdog Transparency International Malaysia (TI-M) raised concerns over the growing use of compounds, settlements and charge withdrawals in corruption-related cases.
Its president, Dr Raymon Ram, said the increasing reliance on such mechanisms without clear public justification could erode trust in anti-corruption institutions and raise questions over accountability.
TI-M called for greater transparency in the exercise of prosecutorial discretion and proposed clearer guidelines governing the use of such mechanisms, particularly in corruption cases.
The organisation also urged stronger oversight and institutional reforms to reinforce public confidence in enforcement decisions.
The Malaysian Corruption Watch (MCW) expressed similar concerns, saying the practice could create a perception that offences involving integrity breaches and abuse of power can be resolved without court proceedings.
On June 3, a former Natural Resources, Environment and Climate Change Ministry officer was freed of a bribery charge involving a gold bracelet after paying a compound to the MACC.
Sessions Court judge Suzana Hussin made the ruling after prosecuting officer A. Selvam informed the court that the accused, Nur Fazreen Kamal, 45, had settled the compound offered by the MACC on May 7.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: MACC, government win appeal to overturn RM900k malicious process judgment
Article:
PUTRAJAYA: The Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC), its officers, and the government have overturned a High Court ruling holding them liable for malicious prosecution against a former company director.
A three-member Court of Appeal bench comprising Justices Datuk Azhahari Kamal Ramli, Datuk Ahmad Kamal Md Shahid, and Datuk Nadzarin Wok Nordin unanimously allowed the appeals, setting aside the High Court's decisions dated Aug 25, 2023, on liability, and May 28, 2024, on the quantum of damages awarded to Nik Mohd Suhaimi Ahmad Ghazali.
Delivering the unanimous decision online, Justice Ahmad Kamal ruled that the tort of malicious prosecution applies only to cases involving the abuse of civil process and does not extend to criminal investigations or criminal proceedings.
"We find that the legal approach taken by the learned High Court judge in extending the tort of malicious process or abuse of civil process to criminal proceedings is unsustainable and inconsistent with prevailing legal developments in the United Kingdom and other Commonwealth jurisdictions," he said.
The appellate court ruled that there was no legal basis for imposing liability on MACC under the tort of malicious process, particularly after the High Court had dismissed Nik Mohd Suhaimi's separate claim for malicious prosecution.
Justice Ahmad Kamal noted that although the High Court found, on a balance of probabilities, that Nik Mohd Suhaimi had established claims involving wrongful imprisonment, arrest and harassment, his claim for malicious prosecution had not succeeded.
He added that any allegation involving abuse of criminal proceedings falls within the scope of the tort of malicious prosecution rather than malicious process.
The Court of Appeal also ordered Nik Mohd Suhaimi to pay RM90,000 in costs.
At the same time, the court struck out Nik Mohd Suhaimi's cross-appeal challenging the dismissal of his malicious prosecution claim on the grounds that it was incompetent and dismissed his appeal seeking a higher award for damages.
Nik Mohd Suhaimi had previously filed a civil suit alleging false imprisonment, wrongful arrest and harassment against MACC and its officers, while also pursuing a claim of malicious prosecution against the Public Prosecutor.
He claimed that he had been investigated for alleged bribery, arrested at the MACC headquarters in Putrajaya and later brought to the commission's office in Kuantan.
According to his claim, he was allegedly harassed, threatened, intimidated and pressured to plead guilty. He also alleged that he was handcuffed and brought before the Sessions Court on forgery charges before later being paraded to the media.
The Sessions Court subsequently acquitted and discharged him without calling for his defence. Although the prosecution initially filed an appeal, it was later withdrawn.
On Aug 25, 2023, the Kuala Lumpur High Court ruled in favour of Nik Mohd Suhaimi on liability and later awarded RM900,000 in damages on May 28, 2024.
However, despite finding that his arrest, detention and prosecution on March 19, 2014, were lawful, the High Court held that the adverse publicity suffered by him caused damage to his life and reputation.
Senior Federal Counsel Liew Horng Bin and Siti Syakimah Ibrahim appeared for MACC and eight other respondents, while Nik Mohd Suhaimi was represented by lawyers Datuk R. Kamalanathan and Vinod Kamalanathan.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Anwar cleared from testifying as witness in RM30mil lawsuit against Vinod Sekhar
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The High Court has allowed an application by Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim to set aside an order requiring him to appear as a subpoenaed witness in a RM30 million civil suit.
The suit was brought by a dozen people against businessman Datuk Vinod Balachandra Sekhar and his wife.
In allowing Anwar's application to set aside the subpoena issued on Jan 15,  High Court judicial commissioner Datuk Muhammad Adam @ Edward Abdullah ruled that the plaintiffs had failed to establish that the prime minister was relevant or material witness in the case and his attendance was necessary.
"No useful purpose would be served by compelling his attendance. To do so would be oppressive and would divert the trial from real legal issues," he said.
The plaintiffs, consisting of 10 investors and two of Vinod's former staff, had in 2023 sued the couple.
They claimed they were misled and suffered losses over alleged technology driven ventures linked to Vinod and his companies between 2002 and 2006 when the projects failed to materialise.
They claimed that Vinod had allegedly invoked Anwar's name to perpetrate fraud and that he was aligned to Anwar by referencing photographs, social media postings and public events involving them both.
"At the highest, such material may show acquaintance or association.
"It does not show the applicant had personal knowledge of the pleaded transactions.
"Relationship does not equate with materiality," said the judicial commissioner.
In his affidavit, Anwar said that he was neither involved nor aware of the alleged dealings, representations and transactions between the plaintiffs and the defendants.
Adam said he found the admission of the fourth plaintiff, Graham David Bell, crucial.
Adam said Bell testified in cross examination that there was not a shred of evidence that Vinod had used Anwar's name to solicit any investment or monies from him or any of the plaintiffs.
"The plaintiffs say the applicant (Anwar) is required because his name was used to perpetrate fraud.
"But their own witness accepted that there was no evidence that his name was used to solicit investments or monies.
"Therefore, the court allows the applicant's bid to set aside the subpoena," he said.
Lawyer Datuk Seri Rajan Navaratnam acted for Vinod, Colin Andrew Pereira appeared for the plaintiffs.
Sanjay Mohan stood for Anwar.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: UNHCR cardholders not immune to Malaysian laws, says Immigration DG
Article:
PORT DICKSON: United Nations High Commissioner for Refugees (UNHCR) cardholders are not immune to enforcement action and remain subject to Malaysian laws if they commit offences.
Immigration Department director-general Datuk Zakaria Shaaban said while valid UNHCR card holders are not detained for immigration documentation offences after verification, they still face action for violating other laws.
"If they do not have any documents, that means it is the department's responsibility; we will detain them and place them in immigration depots, but if they have a UNHCR card, from a human rights perspective, we cannot detain them, we only conduct inspections and so on.
"However, they are subject to other laws. If they commit a crime, for example, the police can take action...if they have a UNHCR card, we can check whether it is genuine or not. If it is genuine, we will release them," he said.
He said this after the passing-out parade for the Basic Immigration Certificate Course and Advanced Immigration Certificate Course Series 1/2026, involving 124 officers here today.
The event was officiated by Home Ministry secretary-general Datuk Dr Awang Alik Jeman.
Zakaria said the Home Ministry is currently conducting a comprehensive registration process for all refugees, including those without UNHCR cards, with the first phase running until the end of the year.
He said the exercise was implemented because the government no longer wanted to rely entirely on data supplied by the UNHCR to determine the actual number of refugees in Malaysia.
"Based on their data, there are more than 200,000 registered refugees involving more than 140 countries, with the majority being Rohingya from Myanmar. We believe there are more refugees in this country than the data supplied by the UNHCR," he said.
Meanwhile, he said the department had detained 33,145 foreigners through 5,430 operations nationwide for various immigration offences as of June 15, with 22,151 placed in immigration depots.
He added that 868 employers, comprising 825 Malaysians and 43 foreigners, had faced action for employing and harbouring illegal immigrants.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Chinese citizen fined RM5,000 for mid-flight theft attempt
Article:
SEPANG: A Chinese citizen was fined RM5,000 by the magistrate's court after pleading guilty to attempting to steal a Japanese passenger's backpack during a flight from Timor-Leste to Kuala Lumpur earlier this week.
Magistrate Khairatul Animah Jelani imposed the fine on Hu Peng, 36, and ordered him to serve 12 months in jail if he fails to pay.
After the proceedings, Hu settled the fine.
Hu was charged with attempting to steal a backpack belonging to a 58-year-old Japanese passenger aboard a flight from Dili at 12.59pm on Monday.
No property was reported missing.
The charge was framed under Section 379 of the Penal Code, read together with Section 511 for attempted theft, which provides for a maximum seven-year jail term, a fine or both upon conviction.
Deputy public prosecutor V. Luvithaa appeared for the prosecution.
Hu and another Chinese national, aged 39, were arrested at KLIA on Monday over the alleged attempted theft aboard the flight.
Police believe the pair may be linked to a syndicate that targets airline passengers while they are asleep during flights.
The second suspect was later made a prosecution witness.
According to investigators, the two men were detained at the KLIA main terminal building after the cabin crew reported that one of them had attempted to steal from the Japanese passenger while he was asleep during the journey.
Initial investigations by Inspector Muhammad Fadzil Zulkifli revealed that cabin crew noticed one of the suspects behaving suspiciously.
He was allegedly seen removing a bag from an overhead compartment that did not belong to him and rummaging through its contents.
When questioned by the cabin crew, the suspect claimed that the bag belonged to him.
However, the crew woke the passenger to verify the ownership of the bag, prompting the suspect to return it to the overhead compartment.
The cabin crew subsequently informed the captain of the incident, who requested airline security personnel to detain the suspects upon the aircraft's arrival at KLIA.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Liftech's IPO oversubscribed by 18.9 times
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Liftech Group Bhd's initial public offering (IPO) was oversubscribed by 18.92 times for the public portion ahead of its ACE Market listing on Bursa Malaysia on June 30.
The company received 5,636 applications for 314.79 million shares from the Malaysian public, compared with the 15.8 million shares offered.
The Bumiputera portion was oversubscribed by 19.45 times, while the non-Bumiputera tranche recorded an oversubscription rate of 18.40 times.
Liftech also said the 7.24 million shares allocated to eligible directors and employees under the pink form allocation were fully subscribed.
Meanwhile, all 72 million shares offered via private placement to Bumiputera investors approved by the Investment, Trade and Industry Ministry and selected investors were fully taken up.
Liftech managing director Bernard Ng said the RM23 million to be raised from the IPO would support the company's next phase of growth, including expanding production capabilities and enhancing project execution capacity.
Under the listing exercise, Liftech will issue 79.23 million new shares at an issue price of 29 sen each, alongside an offer for sale of 15.8 million existing shares to selected investors through private placement.
Based on its enlarged share capital of 314.94 million shares, Liftech is expected to have a market capitalisation of RM91.33 million upon listing.
Liftech intends to allocate the largest portion of its IPO proceeds, RM13.8 million or 59.8 per cent, towards settling bank borrowings incurred from the acquisition of its operational facilities in Penang and Sabah.
The company has also set aside RM1.7 million to acquire new machinery and equipment for its Taiping manufacturing facility, while RM1 million will be used to refurbish and enhance the factory's facilities and office space.
A further RM2 million is earmarked for working capital requirements, primarily to fund the procurement of steel materials, components and accessories used in production activities.
The balance of RM4.5 million will go towards covering listing-related expenses.
M&A Securities Sdn Bhd is the adviser, sponsor, underwriter and placement agent for the IPO, while Wyncorp Advisory Sdn Bhd is the corporate finance adviser.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Cheras student who choked schoolmate over canteen seat suspended
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: A student who allegedly assaulted his schoolmate at a Cheras secondary school in April has been suspended from school.
The assailant, police said, allegedly choked the victim's neck from behind and verbally provoked him.
"The incident occurred in front of the school cooperative and is believed to have stemmed from a misunderstanding over a canteen seating arrangement.
"Both the victim and assailant are Form One students.
"Police received a report on April 16," Cheras police chief Assistant Commissioner Mohd Rosdi Daud said in a statement.
Rosdi said the victim was sent to hospital the same day.
He said a warning and school suspension were imposed on the suspect after a three-party committee meeting involving police, school authorities and guardians was held on April 20.
Yesterday, the victim's father shared a post on Threads saying he was dissatisfied with the punishment imposed on the alleged attacker.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: National Journalists' Day: Celebrating the keepers of truth
Article:
LETTER:
JOURNALISTS are the eyes and ears of our society. Long before the Internet existed, their hard work kept us updated on our nation's progress and major events. They were the unsung heroes who helped shape the country we live in today.
As Malaysia marks National Journalists' Day (Hawana) in Penang tomorrow, it is the ideal moment to reflect on how much this profession has evolved — and why it remains just as important as ever.
In the early days, newsrooms were loud, busy, and full of energy, with everyone racing to meet tight deadlines. Reporters carried nothing more than a notebook, a pen, and used landline phones.
To get their stories, they built strong, personal relationships with their sources. Since they had enough time to carefully check facts, confirm information, and have their work reviewed by several editors, their main focus was always on accuracy and detailed, meaningful storytelling.
At the heart of all this activity were the news editors. Their desks were where rough, unfinished reports were either refined into front-page stories or set aside entirely (spiked).
There was no Google back then, so research meant searching through piles of old newspaper clippings stored in the office libraries. Every day, the whole team worked toward one clear, non-negotiable goal: getting the paper ready for readers the next morning.
Back then, typewriters, notebooks, and landline phones were the only tools available. With longer timeframes to research, verify details, and edit their work, journalists could ensure every story was as precise and reliable as possible.
Over the years, the world of journalism has changed completely. Today's reporters work in a fast-moving digital environment, shaped largely by social media. News no longer waits for the next day's newspaper—it unfolds in real time. Modern journalists need to be quick, comfortable with technology, and ready to publish updates the moment events happen.
While social media lets news reach people instantly, it also makes the job far more challenging.
Now, anyone with a smartphone can post information online, and this has led to a flood of misinformation, fake news, and rumours.
A large part of a journalist's job today involves sorting through all this content to separate fact from fiction and show the public what is really true.
They also face constant pressure to attract readers and gain views, often while working with fewer resources and even shorter deadlines.
Yet even though technology has changed how news is produced and shared, the core purpose of journalism has never shifted.
Whether writing for a traditional newspaper or posting updates on social media, a good journalist's most important duty remains the same: to tell the truth and keep the public well-informed.
This weekend, we extend our gratitude and pay tribute to the veteran journalists who laid the foundations of our media industry. We also thank and appreciate today's reporters, who continue to work hard every day to bring us the news we need. Thank you for everything you do.
TAN SRI LEE LAM THYE
*The views expressed in this article are the author's own and do not necessarily reflect those of the New Straits Times
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: 'Dismantle the stigma': Govt rolls out five-year mental health plan for civil servants
Article:
PUTRAJAYA: The Public Service Department (PSD) today launched its Human Resources Psychology Services Strategic Plan 2026-2030, a five-year roadmap aimed at strengthening the psychological well-being of civil servants.
The plan outlines 12 strategies, 22 programmes and 48 key performance indicators (KPIs) to enhance mental health support across the public service.
PSD said the strategic plan was launched during its June 2026 monthly assembly, themed "R&R (Rest and Treat) Your Soul", which was officiated by Public Service director-general Tan Sri Wan Ahmad Dahlan Abdul Aziz.
PSD also said, one of the key messages delivered by Wan Ahmad Dahlan centred on the concept of "Treat", which encourages civil servants to take proactive intervention measures by overcoming stigma, speaking up about their challenges and seeking professional support when needed.
"The well-being of an organisation starts with the well-being of its people. Let us cultivate a culture of positive support, eliminate the stigma surrounding psychological services and practise effective self-care," it said in a post on its official Facebook page.
"Rest when you are tired and take care of your soul before it deteriorates further."
PSD said the "Treat" concept in the public service refers to proactive intervention measures aimed at addressing employees' mental health and well-being.
The initiative was introduced to complement broader public service reform efforts, including the H.E.M.A.T. work culture framework, which emphasises governance transformation, public empathy, a progressive mindset, appreciation of innovation and transparent administration.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: MICCI calls for greater resilience, agility in tougher global landscape
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Businesses must strengthen resilience, adaptability and public-private cooperation as they navigate a more complex global operating environment, said the Malaysian International Chamber of Commerce and Industry (MICCI).
The operating landscape is being shaped by geopolitical tensions, supply chain disruptions and shifting economic conditions, MICCI said during its 53rd annual general meeting (AGM) and annual luncheon yesterday.
MICCI president Christina Tee said businesses have had to contend with an unprecedented series of disruptions this decade, from the Covid-19 pandemic to shifting trade policies, rising protectionism and ongoing geopolitical tensions.
The conflict in West Asia is now adding further pressure, with ripple effects across global markets impacting energy prices, trade flows, investment decisions, supply chains and operating costs.
"It serves as another reminder that developments far beyond our shores can have direct and far-reaching consequences for businesses in Malaysia," she said.
According to MICCI, businesses are already feeling early impacts through higher logistics costs, rising fuel and energy prices, and increased uncertainty in global markets.
However, the chamber said some of the more significant effects may only become apparent in the coming months or even next year.
"Businesses have learned that uncertainty is no longer the exception. It has become the operating environment," Tee said.
"Resilience is no longer a competitive advantage. It is a necessity."
MICCI noted that Malaysia remains well positioned to benefit from shifting global supply chains, regional economic integration and growing investor interest in Asean.
Tee added that periods of uncertainty can also create opportunities for adaptation, innovation and stronger collaboration between businesses and policymakers.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: TA Securities initiates coverage on DNeX with 'Buy' call
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: TA Securities has initiated coverage of Dagang NeXchange Bhd (DNeX) with a "Buy" recommendation and a target price of 66 sen, implying a potential total return of 71.4 per cent.
The firm said DNeX is at the cusp of an earnings inflection point, driven by its semiconductor and energy businesses, while its IT division continues to provide stable recurring income.
"We forecast the group to return to profitability in the financial year 2026 (FY26), with core profit projected to reach RM80 million.
"Earnings are expected to grow further to RM135.3 million in FY27 and RM166.7 million in FY28," it said.
TA Securities said earnings growth is expected to be driven by SilTerra's capacity expansion, improving utilisation rates and continued growth in average selling prices from richer product mix.
It added that stronger output from the energy segment and increasing contributions from the IT division are also expected to support earnings.
"In our view, the market has yet to fully appreciate SilTerra's strategic position within the rapidly expanding silicon photonics ecosystem, which is benefiting from strong demand driven by artificial intelligence (AI) and data centres.
"At the current share price, DNeX is trading at just 9.9 times FY27 price to earnings ratio, which we believe does not fully reflect its exposure to the AI-driven silicon photonics opportunity, the oil and gas upcycle, and its growing base of recurring digital revenue," it said.
TA Securities said SilTerra's outlook remains favourable, supported by strong growth potential in the silicon photonics segment, which is expected to be a key long-term growth driver amid robust demand from AI and data centre applications.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Teacher among four arrested in drug raid
Article:
BESUT: A teacher was among four men arrested for suspected cannabis distribution following a raid at a house in Kampung Raja yesterday.
District police chief Superintendent Mohd Rozaime Ab Rahim said the 1.30am raid was conducted based on intelligence regarding drug trafficking in the area.
He said police found 21.70g of suspected cannabis, valued at RM1,085, during the inspection.
"All the suspects are aged between 25 and 36, including a civil servant who works as a teacher," he said.
He added that urine screening tests showed three of the suspects tested positive for tetrahydrocannabinol (THC).
The case is being investigated under Section 39A(1) and Section 15(1)(a) of the Dangerous Drugs Act 1952.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Four-vehicle crash leaves motorcyclist injured in George Town
Article:
GEORGE TOWN: A motorcyclist was injured in a four-vehicle crash involving three cars and a motorcycle at the Lorong Batu Lanchang-Thean Teik Highway junction yesterday.
The accident was reported at 11.16am, although the collision is believed to have occurred at 11am.
Northeast district police chief, Assistant Commissioner Abdul Rozak Muhammad, said preliminary investigations found that three vehicles — two cars and a motorcycle — were travelling along Lorong Batu Lanchang when the first car allegedly lost control and rammed into the rear of the second car and the motorcycle.
He said the vehicle then collided with a third car, which was making a right turn from the Thean Teik Highway.
"We believe the crash was caused by the driver of the first car losing control of the vehicle and being unable to stop in time, resulting in a chain collision.
"The driver of the first car, a 22-year-old man from Johor, tested negative for alcohol and drugs.
"The 33-year-old motorcyclist suffered minor injuries, while the drivers of the three cars escaped unhurt," he said today.
Videos and images of the incident have since been widely shared on social media.
Passers-by were seen checking on the 22-year-old driver, who appeared visibly shaken following the incident.
The case is being investigated under Section 43(1) of the Road Transport Act 1987 for careless and inconsiderate driving.
If found guilty, the driver may be fined between RM5,000 and RM10,000 and/or jailed for up to 12 months.
A conviction under the section will also be recorded on the offender's driving licence.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: SIS Forum plans bill to tackle unpaid spousal, child support
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Non-governmental organisation SIS Forum is set to collaborate with key stakeholders to draft a comprehensive proposal bill aimed at ensuring people do not evade their financial responsibilities toward their spouses, ex-spouses, and children.
Its executive director, Rozana Isa, said that the organisation will conduct rigorous research to evaluate the efficacy of the current system and identify critical areas for improvement.
"We endeavour to engage and work collaboratively with as many people, communities and NGOs as possible," she told the New Straits Times.
This initiative follows data from Telenisa, SIS Forum's free legal advice service which specifically helps people navigate Islamic family law.
Between 2016 and 2025, Telenisa recorded a total of 4,761 cases, out of which 2,110 reports were tied to child-related issues.
According to Telenisa Statistics and Findings 2025 report published yesterday, 1,000 of those cases involved child maintenance (367 cases) and its arrears (633 cases).
Rozana added that SIS Forum will engage with the government to gather data to ensure the bill is drafted effectively.
"Government engagement goes without saying. This issue affects all families in Malaysia.
"We need to do the necessary to understand better what is required for what we have now and what is working well, working but not great or not working at all.
"We have some answers but we need to know more," she said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Thunderstorms, heavy rain warning issued for several states until 5pm
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) has issued a thunderstorm warning with heavy rain and strong winds expected in several areas across Peninsular Malaysia, Sabah and Sarawak until 5pm today.
In a weather warning issued this afternoon, MetMalaysia said the entire states of Perlis, Penang and Perak are expected to be affected by adverse weather conditions.
In Kedah, the warning covers Kubang Pasu, Kota Setar, Yan, Kuala Muda, Baling, Kulim and Bandar Baharu.
The affected areas in Kelantan are Jeli, Machang, Pasir Puteh, Kuala Krai and Gua Musang, while in Terengganu, the warning covers Besut, Setiu and Hulu Terengganu.
In Pahang, thunderstorms and heavy rain are forecast in Cameron Highlands, Lipis, Raub, Bentong and Rompin.
Several districts in Selangor are also expected to experience adverse weather, namely Sabak Bernam, Hulu Selangor, Gombak, Petaling, Hulu Langat and Sepang, as well as the federal territories of Kuala Lumpur and Putrajaya.
In Negri Sembilan, the warning covers Seremban, Port Dickson and Rembau, while Segamat is the only district affected in Johor.
In Sarawak, the affected areas are Lundu in Kuching Division, Simunjan in Samarahan Division and parts of Betong Division.
For Sabah, the warning involves Kuala Penyu, Beaufort and Tambunan in the Interior Division, the West Coast Division, as well as Kota Marudu and Kudat in the Kudat Division.
MetMalaysia said the warning was issued when there are signs of thunderstorms with rainfall intensity exceeding 20 mm per hour, which are expected to last for more than an hour.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Notice on Hamzah as opposition leader to be sent before next parliament sitting
Article:
KOTA BARU: A formal notification to Dewan Rakyat Speaker on the appointment of Datuk Seri Hamzah Zainudin as opposition leader will be submitted before the next parliamentary sitting.
Perikatan Nasional (PN) information chief Tan Sri Annuar Musa said the notification could be delivered at any time, adding that Hamzah's appointment had already received overwhelming support from opposition lawmakers.
"The notification to the Speaker regarding Hamzah's appointment as opposition leader will be conveyed before the next Dewan Rakyat sitting.
"It can be submitted at any time," he told reporters here today.
Annuar said the appointment of an opposition leader was determined by opposition members of parliament, in the same way that a prime minister was chosen by government lawmakers.
"The opposition leader is decided by opposition MPs, who choose among themselves a person who receives majority support from opposition lawmakers.
"Likewise, the prime minister is appointed from among government MPs based on majority support within the ruling bloc. That has always been the practice," he said.
He dismissed suggestions that party hierarchy or positions within political parties should determine who leads the opposition bloc in Parliament.
Annuar said Hamzah was appointed after securing the backing of almost all opposition MPs, except six.
"All opposition MPs, except six, agreed that Hamzah should be the opposition leader. This includes MPs from Bersatu, where 19 of them supported him.
"All 43 Pas MPs also agreed that Hamzah should become the opposition leader. Therefore, he is the opposition leader," he said.
Dewan Rakyat Speaker Tan Sri Dr Johari Abdul recently said Hamzah's position as opposition leader has yet to be officially recognised.
He said the House has not
received any new notification
from PN regarding a change in the post.
According to the parliamentary calendar for the Fifth Session of the 15th Parliament, the first meeting runs from Jan 19 to March 3, while the second meeting is scheduled from June 22 to July 16.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: PKR leaves Rafizi, Nik Nazmi matter to legal team
Article:
JOHOR BARU: PKR has left any decision on possible action against former Pandan MP Datuk Seri Rafizi Ramli and former Setiawangsa MP Nik Nazmi Nik Ahmad to its legal team following their departure from the party.
Following the move, PKR said it would seek legal advice on enforcing the RM10 million bond signed by the duo after they
vacated their seats
and quit the party.
Secretary-general Datuk Dr Fuziah Salleh said the party's lawyers would determine the next course of action involving the former PKR deputy president and former vice-president.
"They have already resigned and left the party. So I leave the matter to the legal team," she said after visiting the Program Jualan Rahmah Madani here today.
On May 17, Rafizi and Nik Nazmi vacated their
parliamentary seats
before announcing their exit from PKR.
Rafizi and Nik Nazmi subsequently registered as ordinary members of Parti Bersama Malaysia on May 21.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: PN Supreme Council to meet June 22 over logo use, candidate endorsements
Article:
KOTA BARU: The Perikatan Nasional Supreme Council is expected to meet on June 22 to discuss several unresolved issues, including the use of the logo and candidate endorsements for upcoming elections.
PN information chief Tan Sri Annuar Musa said matters involving Pas would be decided by the party itself, while issues concerning PN must be deliberated and resolved at the coalition level.
"There will be a PN Supreme Council meeting, if I'm not mistaken, on June 22.
"Anything related to Pas is decided by Pas but matters concerning PN must be decided through PN meetings.
"We cannot rush into holding a PN meeting. We need to consult lawyers first because there are legal issues involved that require careful study before any decision can be made," he told reporters here today.
Annuar, who is also a Pas central committee member, said no final decision had been reached on the matter and that all parties must adhere to existing regulations.
"We are still managing the issue and cannot provide a definitive answer yet. No decision has been made," he said.
Annuar said the endorsement of PN candidates would also be discussed at the meeting, stressing that the coalition comprises four component parties and decisions must be reached through consensus.
"The purpose of the meeting is to find solutions to issues within PN through consultation and mutual agreement.
"PN is not a single political party like Pas that has over a million members, but PN consists of four component parties.
"Therefore, all four parties must sit together and discuss matters before any decision is made," he said.
On plans by Bersatu to use the coalition's symbol in Johor and Negri Sembilan, Annuar said the procedure remains clear that those wishing to use the logo must obtain approval.
"Only the PN chairman can endorse the use of the coalition's logo and sign the letter of authorisation for candidates, similar to the practice in Barisan Nasional," he said.
Bersatu president Tan Sri Muhyiddin Yassin recently said the party would
remain in PN
and contest the Johor and Negri Sembilan elections using the coalition's logo.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Malaysia, global firms ramp up AI use across key business units
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Companies globally, including those in Malaysia, are accelerating their adoption of artificial intelligence (AI), as businesses increasingly deploy the technology to reshape operations, improve efficiency and unlock new growth opportunities.
AI is being embedded across a broad range of functions — from automation and data analytics to customer engagement and decision-support systems — as organisations move beyond pilot projects and integrate the technology into core operating models.
"In Malaysia, companies across financial services, healthcare, manufacturing and logistics are steadily deploying AI solutions to streamline workflows, enhance productivity and support more data-driven decision-making," said technology expert Sam Goh.
He added that the emphasis is increasingly shifting towards practical applications, particularly in predictive analytics, intelligent automation and digital customer services.
Meanwhile, technology firms in the United Arab Emirates (UAE) are positioning themselves as regional leaders in AI adoption, supported by strong government backing and national strategies aimed at developing advanced digital economies.
Goh said the trend reflects a structural shift, with AI evolving into a foundational layer of modern business rather than a standalone innovation, as companies seek to improve efficiency, reduce costs and generate new revenue streams.
Against this backdrop, Nasdaq-listed UAE-based Robo.ai Inc has entered into an agreement to acquire 100 per cent of QC Capital Ltd for US$60 million.
The acquisition marks a strategic step in Robo.ai's development of a global AI robotics network platform.
Chief executive Benjamin Zhai said the company is building an integrated AI robotics ecosystem for the next generation of the intelligent economy, with QC Capital expected to enhance capabilities in AI-driven investment decision-making, data asset development, venture building, M&A integration and global networks.
"Following the completion of the transaction, QC Capital is expected to serve as Robo.ai's platform for strategic holdings, venture building, investment development and data asset growth, supporting the company's continued expansion across artificial intelligence, robotics, digital infrastructure, smart cities, intelligent mobility, low-altitude economy and the next-generation digital economy," he said.
QC Capital operates as an AI-focused technology holding and venture-building platform spanning technology development, incubation and industrial investment.
For Robo.ai, the acquisition would strengthen its ability to source and scale technology companies, building on previous deals including Neurovia AI, a visual data processing and compression technology firm.
The integration is expected to enhance capabilities in capital deployment, venture incubation, cross-border M&A, post-investment management and global commercialisation.
Under the agreement, consideration shares will be issued and released over an eight-year period, subject to the achievement of agreed revenue milestones, including a cumulative target of about US$2.4 billion for 2026 and 2027, which will serve as a key benchmark for phased vesting.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: KPJ strengthens preventive healthcare strategy
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: KPJ Healthcare Bhd is strengthening its focus on preventive healthcare with the launch of a nationwide campaign to raise awareness of digestive health and promote the early detection of gastrointestinal diseases.
Under its Digestive Care 4 Life campaign, the hospital operator will provide 500 complimentary endoscopy and colonoscopy procedures to eligible patients referred by the Health Ministry facilities to participating KPJ hospitals nationwide.
The initiative, launched by Health Minister Datuk Seri Dr Dzulkefly Ahmad, supports broader efforts to expand access to screening services and encourage earlier intervention for conditions such as colorectal cancer.
Digestive diseases, including colorectal cancer, often develop with few or no symptoms in their early stages, limiting opportunities for timely diagnosis and treatment. Regular screening can help identify abnormalities sooner, allowing patients to receive appropriate medical care before conditions progress.
KPJ Healthcare president and managing director Chin Keat Chyuan said the campaign reflects the group's commitment to improving health outcomes through prevention and early intervention while reinforcing its role in advancing community health beyond hospital-based treatment.
"Many digestive conditions can be managed more effectively when identified early, yet barriers such as cost, limited awareness and hesitation often delay individuals from seeking appropriate assessments," he said in a statement.
He added that the campaign is designed to encourage Malaysians to take a more proactive approach to managing their health and to seek timely screening before conditions become more severe and difficult to treat.
This initiative goes beyond raising awareness; it aims to assist individuals in taking the necessary steps for further evaluation. In line with our Care for Life purpose, we remain committed to ensuring that healthcare is accessible and inclusive, particularly for communities that may face additional challenges in seeking care," Chin said.
In conjunction with the campaign, KPJ Healthcare also announced a community outreach programme in collaboration with the Malaysian Federation of the Deaf and Hear Me Malaysia, which will provide 100 complimentary health screenings for members of the deaf community.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Scientists discover 'hyperparasite' in Borneo jungle
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysian scientists have discovered a new species of parasitic fungus in Borneo that preys on the "zombie fungus" known to kill insects.
The new species was collected during field trips conducted by the Universiti Malaysia Sabah Institute for Tropical Biology and Conservation.
It was dubbed a "hyperparasite" because it "effectively parasitises the primary pathogen," said the institute's deputy director Jaya Seelan Sathiya Seelan.
"The fungus belongs to the genus Pleurocordyceps and acts as a specialised hyperparasite," Seelan said.
The new species targets ants already infected by Ophiocordyceps, or "zombie fungus", which manipulates the infected insect's nervous system and makes it behave erratically before killing it and bursting from its carcass.
"Rather than manipulating the insect's nervous system itself, Pleurocordyceps infiltrates and feeds directly on the thriving Ophiocordyceps tissue inside the host," Seelan said.
Named after its unique, distinctly horn-shaped structure, Pleurocordyceps cornusynnemata was discovered after scientists studied a dead ant collected from the Danum Valley, a remote area in southern Sabah.
The discovery was also published in Phytotaxa, the leading journal in taxonomic botany, in April.
It is not the first hyperparasite of its kind, but "it is the world's first known member of its genus to feature this highly distinct horn-shaped structure", Seelan said.
During the field trips, scientists also discovered a new species of spider-killing fungus, which spreads spores through the arachnid before killing it.
"These newly documented fungi hold immense potential... both as sources for developing next-generation antimicrobial drugs and as highly effective biocontrol agents against agricultural pests," Seelan said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: PKR candidate list for Johor, NS state elections 99pct complete  – Fuziah
Article:
JOHOR BARU: PKR's list of candidates for the Johor and Negri Sembilan state elections is 99 per cent final, said party secretary-general Datuk Dr Fuziah Salleh.
She said PKR would contest 20 state seats in Johor and 16 in Negri Sembilan, with the official candidate announcement expected in the next few days.
"We will present a balanced combination of experienced and new faces, including women and young people. The percentage of new faces will be revealed on announcement day," she told reporters after attending the Rahmah Sales Programme at Econsave Taman Daya here today.
The Election Commission (EC) has set June 27 as nomination day for the Johor state election, with polling scheduled for July 11 after the state assembly was dissolved on June 1.
The Johor assembly has 56 seats. Before dissolution, Barisan Nasional (BN) held 40 seats, Pakatan Harapan (PH) 12, Perikatan Nasional (PN) three and MUDA one.
Meanwhile, the Negri Sembilan state election will be held on August 1, with nomination day on July 18 and early voting on July 28.
The Negri Sembilan assembly, which has 36 seats, was dissolved on June 5. Before dissolution, PH held 17 seats, BN 14 and PN five.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Price volatility keeps India demand modest, China flips to discount
Article:
Gold demand was modest in India this week as prices fell to their lowest level in two-and-a-half months and remained volatile, while top consumer China flipped to a discount for the first time since late December.
Dealers in India quoted discounts of up to US$54 an ounce over official domestic prices this week, inclusive of 15 per cent import and 3 per cent sales levies, widening from last week's discounts of up to US$35.
"The price correction is helping bring buyers back to the market, but excessive volatility is prompting some buyers to wait for a clearer price trend," said an Ahmedabad-based jeweller.
Domestic gold prices fell to 146,252 rupees per 10 grams on Friday, the lowest since April 2.
"Investment demand has remained weak over the past few weeks. However, jewellers are showing some interest in building inventories," said a Mumbai-based bullion dealer with a private bank.
India's physically backed gold exchange-traded funds (ETFs) recorded their first net monthly outflow in a year in May, driven by profit-taking following a price rally on higher import duties.
In China, bullion shifted to discounts of US$4 to US$8 an ounce to the global benchmark spot price
, compared with premiums of US$1 to US$5 last week, as investors stayed on the sidelines awaiting further details on the US-Iran deal.
Gold prices have fallen over 23 per cent since the start of the US-Israeli war against Iran in late February, pressured by fears of energy-driven inflation and expectations of higher US interest rates.
"The physical gold market in Shanghai remains very quiet, and I am not seeing much buying interest. Investors across China are still concerned about uncertainty in the Middle East and are waiting for a clearer picture," said Peter Fung, head of dealing at Wing Fung Precious Metals.
"Demand may pick up after the holidays or maybe in July or August."
Markets in mainland China and Hong Kong were closed for the Dragon Boat Festival holiday on Friday.
In Hong Kong, gold traded between par and a US$2 premium, while in Japan, it was sold at a discount of US$0.25.
In Singapore, gold was sold between a US$0.50 discount and a US$1.80 premium. (Reporting by Noel John in Bengaluru and Rajendra Jadhav in Mumbai; Editing by Eileen Soreng)
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Bentong trailer driver freed on bail pending probe into fatal Karak Expressway crash [WATCH]
Article:
PEKAN: The trailer driver involved in a fatal crash that killed four people in Bentong on June 14 has been released on police bail pending prosecution.
The 40-year-old suspect was remanded for four days starting June 15 after a urine screening confirmed that he had
consumed methamphetamine.
Pahang police chief Datuk Seri Yahaya Othman said the driver is being investigated for causing death while driving under the influence of drugs and for drug consumption.
"The driver's remand order, which began on Monday, expired yesterday (June 18), and he has since been released.
"The Bentong Traffic Enforcement and Investigation Department and the Narcotics Crime Investigation Department are wrapping up their investigations," he told reporters at the Pekan convention hall today.
Earlier, Yahaya attended the memorandum of understanding handover ceremony between the Malaysian Islamic Economic Development Foundation (YaPEIM) and the Pahang police contingent.
Meanwhile, Yahaya said police are awaiting the completion of laboratory reports before referring the case to the deputy public prosecutor's Office for further action.
The 4.25pm crash along the Kuala Lumpur–Karak Expressway claimed the lives of four people, including an infant, after the car they were travelling in was
crushed
by an overturned trailer.
The victims — a man, two women, and a 1-year-old child — were killed on the spot, while the lorry driver sustained head and hip injuries.
On June 15, the victims' family called for
stern action
against the lorry driver, saying that similar incidents involving drug-positive drivers have occurred repeatedly.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: PKR to contest Puteri Wangsa state seat despite Johor Amanah claims
Article:
JOHOR BARU: PKR will contest the Puteri Wangsa state seat in the upcoming Johor election despite state Amanah's claims over the seat.
PKR secretary-general Datuk Dr Fuziah Salleh said discussions will be held soon at the highest leadership level to resolve the dispute.
"It will be resolved at the highest level. That is the claim by Johor Amanah, but it will be settled at the highest level," she said when commenting on the matter today.
She said this during a press conference after a walkabout at the Madani Rahmah Sale Programme at the Econsave Cash and Carry supermarket in Taman Daya here.
Recently, Johor Amanah issued a statement opposing the decision to hand over the Puteri Wangsa state seat to PKR for the upcoming state election.
Johor Amanah vice-chairman Dr Zuhan Zain stated that the state chapter had never agreed to cede the seat.
He said Johor Amanah had made early preparations to reclaim and defend the Puteri Wangsa seat, which was loaned to the Malaysian United Democratic Alliance (Muda) during the 2022 Johor state election.
Zuhan added that the state chapter took note of media reports regarding the status of the seat, noting that there might have been a misinterpretation or confusion over statements issued by certain parties.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Inflation in Malaysia edges up to 2.0pct, still one of Asia's lowest rates
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Inflation in Malaysia expanded to two per cent in May from 1.8 per cent in April, driven mainly by higher prices for communications, food and beverages, housing-related expenses and recreation.
The Statistics Department said the Consumer Price Index (CPI) had risen to 137.1 points last month from 134.4 a year earlier, while headline inflation increased by 0.1 per cent month-on-month.
Despite the uptick, Malaysia continued to record one of the region's lower inflation rates.
At 2.0 per cent, the country's inflation remained below that of the Philippines (6.8 per cent), Vietnam (5.6 per cent), South Korea (3.1 per cent), Indonesia (3.1 per cent) and Thailand (2.8 per cent), although it was higher than China's 1.2 per cent.
Chief statistician Datuk Seri Dr Mohd Uzir Mahidin said inflation in the information and communication group rose 2.1 per cent, followed by food and beverages (1.4 per cent), housing, water, electricity, gas and other fuels (1.2 per cent) and recreation, sport and culture (1.1 per cent).
The food and beverages category, which accounts for 29.8 per cent of the CPI basket, recorded faster growth of 1.4 per cent in May compared with 1.2 per cent in April.
Among food categories, prices for fish and seafood as well as sugar, confectionery and desserts increased, while vegetables and dairy products continued to decline.
Chicken prices remained lower year-on-year, with standard chicken averaging RM10.48 per kilogramme (kg) in May compared with RM10.63 per kg a year earlier.
Housing, water, electricity, gas and other fuels inflation edged up to 1.2 per cent from 1.1 per cent in April, supported by higher maintenance and repair costs.
Electricity, gas and fuel prices remained in negative territory, although the decline moderated to 3.1 per cent from 4.5 per cent previously.
Transport inflation eased to 3.8 per cent from 4.1 per cent in April, reflecting slower increases in public transport and personal transport operating costs.
Average RON97 petrol prices fell to RM4.81 per litre from RM5.06 in April, while diesel prices in Peninsular Malaysia declined to RM5.01 per litre from RM5.92.
At the state level, eight states recorded inflation above the national average, led by Pahang (2.8 per cent), Negri Sembilan (2.6 per cent), Labuan (2.6 per cent) and Johor (2.5 per cent). Sarawak registered the lowest inflation rate at 0.5 per cent.
For food inflation, Johor recorded the highest increase at 2.7 per cent, followed by Melaka, Negri Sembilan and Pahang at 2.3 per cent each.
The Statistics Department noted that 63.5 per cent of items in the CPI basket recorded price increases in May, although 97.5 per cent of those increases were 10 per cent or less.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Nine arrested in probe over fake medical certificates in Pekan
Article:
PEKAN: Four more people have been arrested over the sale and purchase of fake medical certificates, bringing the total number of suspects to nine.
Pahang police chief Datuk Seri Yahaya Othman said all nine suspects, who were previously remanded, will be released on police bail today.
"The nine consist of a nurse, alleged middlemen, and several individuals believed to have purchased the fake certificates. We have recorded all their statements.
"Their remand ends today and they will be released. We will complete the investigation papers before submitting them to the deputy public prosecutor's office for further action," he told reporters at the Pekan Convention Hall today.
Earlier, Yahaya attended a memorandum of understanding handover ceremony between the Malaysian Islamic Economic Development Foundation (YaPEIM) and the Pahang police contingent.
Yahaya said the syndicate is believed to have started selling the fake certificates last year, and police are tracking down other potential suspects.
Five people, including the nurse, were initially r
emanded
on June 15, followed by another four suspects in their 40s on June 17.
Investigations revealed the syndicate sold fake medical certificates bearing the name, stamp, and signature of a government
medical officer
who had actually left the respective facility in 2023.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: New mosque to rise in front of TRX as Bukit Bintang seeks permanent worship space
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR — A new mosque to be known as Masjid Warisan is being planned on a site in front of the Tun Razak Exchange (TRX) development, with the project expected to become a new landmark in the capital.
Minister in the Prime Minister’s Department (Religious Affairs) Zulkifli Hassan said the initiative reflects efforts to strengthen Islamic facilities in the Bukit Bintang area, Malay daily
Kosmo! Online
reported today.
“Insya-Allah, we want to develop a mosque that is beautiful and outstanding, in line with the grandeur of the TRX Tower,” he was quoted as saying after Friday prayers at Oasis Outreach Bukit Bintang, a temporary worship place that opened today while permanent facilities are being developed.
He said the project involves a 5.6-acre site located opposite TRX.
The planned mosque will be developed in collaboration with the Federal Territories Islamic Religious Council (MAIWP), Kuala Lumpur City Hall (DBKL) and other relevant agencies.
Zulkifli said the project is expected to become a new landmark in Kuala Lumpur.
He said the mosque will be built following agreement from the owner of a building previously known as the Yayasan Selangor building to dedicate the premises for religious use.
The owner has agreed to waqf the property despite its potential commercial value, he said.
“Alhamdulillah, we have agreed to build a mosque on the site that can accommodate more than 1,500 worshippers. The property owner has also agreed to endow it, and MAIWP has agreed to fund the project,” he said.
Zulkifli said the mosque will help address the lack of dedicated worship facilities in Bukit Bintang, a major commercial and tourism hub.
He said the area currently does not have a mosque specifically serving Friday prayers for the local community and visitors.

Title: Low water levels at major dams trigger cloud seeding and fire warning in Sarawak
Article:
KUCHING, June 19 — The water level at Batang Ai Dam is currently at ‘Event and Drought Emergency Level 1’, while Bakun Dam is at ‘Drought Emergency Level 1’, revealed Datuk Amar Douglas Uggah Embas.
The Deputy Chief Minister said Murum Dam is currently under ‘Drought Event’ status, while water levels at other major dams remain within normal ranges but could deteriorate if the hot and dry weather persists.
He said the Sarawak government has launched another round of cloud seeding operations in collaboration with the National Disaster Management Agency (Nadma) focussing on major dams and water catchment areas across the state.
A Sarawak Public Communication Unit (Ukas) report quoted Uggah as saying the targeted dams are Batang Ai in Lubok Antu, Bakun and Murum dams in Belaga, as well as the Bengoh, Matang, Simunjan, and Gerugu catchment areas.
“We hope the operation will be successful so that water supply problems can be resolved or at least reduced. The weather conditions are indeed very hot and dry at the moment,” he told a press conference here on the cloud seeding operation.
Uggah, who is also State Disaster Management Committee (SDMC) chairman, said a similar operation conducted in May had successfully increased water levels at Batang Ai Dam, although water levels at Bakun Dam remained a concern.
He stressed that maintaining adequate water levels at Batang Ai, Bakun, and Murum dams is crucial as the three facilities serve as Sarawak’s main sources of electricity generation.
He also assured the public that the cloud seeding process is safe as it uses ordinary salt as the seeding agent.
Separately, Uggah said SDMC has directed all divisional and district disaster management committees to strengthen their readiness to deal with potential haze and peatland fires as the dry spell continues.
He said rising temperatures and prolonged dry conditions typically lead to an increase in hotspots, heightening the risk of open burning and peatland fires.
“We have instructed all divisional disaster management committees chaired by residents, as well as district committees headed by district officers, to make early preparations.
“Among the key areas of focus are locations identified as being prone to fires,” he said, citing Kuala Baram as among areas being closely monitored.
Uggah said the state government has put in place various supporting infrastructure, including tube wells, small dams, and observation towers to facilitate rapid response efforts in the event of fires.
He added that cooperation with landowners is also being strengthened to ensure firefighting operations can be carried out more effectively.
The Department of Environment and the Natural Resources and Environment Board (NREB) have also been instructed to deploy drones to monitor high-risk peatland areas.
“The wisest approach is to act as soon as smoke is detected and not wait until flames become visible.
“Once flames can be seen, the affected area is usually already extensive and the fire can spread rapidly because peatland becomes extremely dry and highly combustible,” he said.
Should the need arise, he said SDMC would implement flooding operations in peatland areas in accordance with established standard operating procedures to prevent fires from spreading further.
Uggah also reminded the public to refrain from open burning and to immediately report any sign of smoke or fire to the authorities. — The Borneo Post

Title: Teacher among four arrested on suspicion of using Besut house as drug den
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, June 19 — Police have arrested four men, including a teacher, after raiding a house suspected of being used for drug-related activities in Kampung Raja, Besut early yesterday.
According to
Sinar Harian
, Besut police chief Superintendent Mohd Rozaime Ab Rahim said the raid was carried out at about 1.30am by the Besut district police headquarters’ Narcotics Criminal Investigation Division following intelligence on suspected drug distribution in the area.
“All the suspects arrested are aged between 25 and 36. Initial investigations found that one of them is a civil servant serving as a teacher,” he was quoted as saying in a statement yesterday.
Police also seized 21.70g of suspected cannabis, estimated to be worth RM1,085, during the raid.
Mohd Rozaime said initial urine screening found that three of the four suspects tested positive for Tetrahydrocannabinol, or THC.
All four suspects have been detained, with the case being investigated under Sections 39A(1) and 15(1)(a) of the Dangerous Drugs Act 1952.
He also urged the public to continue providing information on drug-related offences in their areas to help safeguard community safety.

Title: High Court sets aside subpoena for Anwar to testify in RM30m civil suit against businessman and wife
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, June 19 — The High Court here has set aside a subpoena requiring Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim to appear as a witness in a RM30 million civil suit filed by 12 individuals against businessman Datuk Vinod Balachandra Sekhar and his wife, Winny Yeap Liew Heoh.
The suit was filed in 2023 by 10 investors and two former employees of Vinod, who alleged misrepresentation, fraud and breach of contract.
Judicial Commissioner Datuk Muhammad Adam @ Edward Abdullah, in allowing Anwar’s application to set aside the subpoena issued on January 15, ruled that the plaintiffs had failed to establish that the Prime Minister was a relevant and material witness to the proceedings.
“No useful purpose would be served by compelling his attendance. To do so would be oppressive and would divert the trial from the real legal issues.
“The plaintiffs relied on photographs, social media postings and public appearances involving Anwar and Vinod. At their highest, such materials may demonstrate acquaintance or association, but they do not establish that the applicant possesses personal knowledge of the transactions pleaded.
“Relationship does not equate to materiality,” he said.
The court further emphasised that the issuance of a subpoena is not automatic and must be subject to judicial control.
“Otherwise, anyone mentioned in an email, photographed with a party, or referred to in a conversation could be compelled to attend court. That is not the law,” the judge said.
Muhammad Adam also noted that although the plaintiffs argued Anwar’s name had allegedly been used to perpetrate fraud, their own witness acknowledged there was no evidence showing the Prime Minister’s name had been used to solicit investments or obtain funds.
“Therefore, the court allows the applicants’ application to set aside the subpoena,” he ruled.
The court also ordered the plaintiffs to pay RM20,000 in costs to Anwar.
Lawyer Sanjay Mohan appeared for the Prime Minister, while lawyer Colin Andrew Pereira represented the plaintiffs.
According to the plaintiffs’ claim, Vinod had persuaded them to invest either personally or through his companies without disclosing his alleged bankruptcy status.
The plaintiffs further alleged that concerns raised in an email dated September 30, 2020, were dismissed by Vinod, who allegedly stated that an individual named “Anwar” would resolve the matter, leading them to believe the reference was to the Prime Minister. — Bernama

Title: Chief Justice: Public trust is judiciary’s most valuable asset, must be earned
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, June 19 — Public trust remains the judiciary’s most valuable asset and must be earned through integrity, accountability and consistent conduct, said Chief Justice Tun Wan Ahmad Farid Wan Salleh.
Drawing on the legacy of former Chief Justice Tun Zaki Azmi, he said institutional leaders must be prepared to make difficult decisions and work to leave behind stronger, more trusted institutions for future generations.
“Public trust is not inherited or assumed, but earned gradually through consistent conduct and integrity in judicial outcomes across every level of the court system.
“He (Tun Zaki) earned it, he protected it and he left this institution more worthy of it than he found it,” he said in his speech at the inaugural Tun Zaki Azmi Lecture titled ‘Leading with Courage, Stewarding Justice’ at the Asian International Arbitration Centre (AIAC) here today.
The nation’s top judge said that everyone entrusted with leadership responsibilities should recognise the value of their contributions, noting that decisions made behind the scenes, often under pressure and without recognition, play an important role in strengthening institutions.
“I want to say this to every person in this room who carries a responsibility, who has felt the weight of institutional leadership, who has asked themselves whether they are leaving things better than they found them: your work matters.
“The decisions you make in quiet rooms, under pressure, without applause, matter. And the wisdom you have built through those decisions is not yours alone to keep. It belongs to us. To this community. To the next generation of leaders who will inherit what we build here, and who deserve to inherit something worthy of their dedication,” he said.
Elaborating on the objectives of the lecture series, he said the Tun Zaki Lectures on Leadership and Stewardship and the Leadership and Stewardship Research Initiative were intended to promote meaningful dialogue on leadership and develop practical scholarship relevant to the realities of institutional leadership.
“The Tun Zaki Lectures on Leadership and Stewardship are not a ceremonial series. They are a serious, sustained effort to create a living dialogue between those who have led and those who are preparing to lead, to bring experience and principle into conversation with each other, in a space that takes both seriously.
“Meanwhile, the Leadership and Stewardship Research Initiative will do the longer, deeper work alongside it, building scholarship that is rigorous, honest, and directly relevant to the realities of leading institutions under pressure. Not theory for its own sake, but knowledge we can actually use and pass on,” he said.
The public lecture platform is named in recognition of the sixth Chief Justice of Malaysia, Tun Zaki Azmi, for his continuing contributions to leadership, stewardship and institutional responsibility, and serves as the founding platform for the Leadership and Stewardship Research Initiative.
The Leadership and Stewardship Initiative is a long-term platform dedicated to advancing leadership, stewardship, governance and public trust.
Founded on the belief that leadership lessons should be preserved and shared, the initiative seeks to capture the wisdom of distinguished leaders and transform it into lasting knowledge for future generations. — Bernama

Title: Court sentences grandfather to 33 years jail, 14 strokes for raping and sexually assaulting his granddaughters
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, June 19 — A 67-year-old man was sentenced to 33 years’ jail and 14 strokes of the cane by the Sessions Court in Muar today after pleading guilty to rape and sexual assault involving two of his granddaughters, aged 12 and nine.
According to
Harian Metro
, judge Mohd Khairi Haron handed down the sentence after accepting the accused’s guilty plea to five charges.
The man, who has three children, was accused in the first and fourth charges of committing sexual assault against his 12-year-old granddaughter at a house in Batu Pahat.
The offences were framed under Section 14(a) of the Sexual Offences Against Children Act 2017, read together with Section 16 of the same Act.
For the second and third charges, he was accused of raping the same victim at the house between January and December last year, under Section 376(2)(d) of the Penal Code.
The fifth charge involved the sexual assault of another granddaughter, aged nine, at the same location last year.
Deputy public prosecutor Nur Ameerah Allaudeen prosecuted, while the accused was unrepresented.
The court also ordered him to undergo counselling while in prison and to be placed under police supervision for three years after completing his sentence, in line with Sections 26 and 27 of the Sexual Offences Against Children Act.

Title: Selangor hawkers to get six-month rent discount from July under state relief package
Article:
SHAH ALAM, June 19 — The Selangor government will provide a 30 per cent rental reduction for hawkers and traders operating at Local Authority (PBT) premises under Phase Two of the Selangor Resilience Strengthening Package.
Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Amirudin Shari said the initiative aims to help entrepreneurs sustain their businesses without passing increased operating costs on to consumers through higher food prices.
The rental reduction will be implemented for six months beginning this July.
“This assistance will benefit 21,234 traders with an allocation of RM4.5 million from the state government.
“The reduction will be granted automatically without requiring any application from traders,” he said when tabling the package at a special sitting of the Selangor Legislative Assembly today.
Amirudin said that under the same package, the state government will also introduce a three-month moratorium for 1,012 tenants of the Selangor Smart Sewa scheme beginning in October.
He said the moratorium will be coordinated by the Selangor Housing and Property Board (LPHS) based on tenant applications to ensure eligible participants continue to enjoy affordable rental rates of between RM450 and RM1,300 per month.
“To assist civil servants currently repaying housing loans under the State Government Housing Loan Scheme, this administration will also offer a three-month moratorium on repayments from October to December 2026.
“This assistance is expected to benefit 2,333 civil servants in Selangor, subject to applications,” he said.
Meanwhile, Amirudin said the state government is also encouraging the transition towards clean and sustainable energy by offering a 100 per cent assessment tax rebate for 2026 to homeowners who install green technology systems.
Eligible technologies include rooftop solar panels, rainwater harvesting systems, recycling facilities, food waste composting systems and electric vehicle charging units.
He said the initiative is expected to support Selangor’s long-term aspiration of becoming a carbon-neutral state.
“Each PBT will allocate funds ranging from RM100,000 to RM1.5 million respectively for district councils, municipal councils and city councils, with applications to be coordinated and simplified at the local authority level for the benefit of the people,” he said.
Phase Two of the Selangor Resilience Strengthening Package involves 15 initiatives worth RM209.26 million as part of the state government’s strategic response to global energy uncertainty linked to developments in West Asia.
Amirudin said the measures go beyond direct financial aid and focus on broader economic empowerment to ensure the benefits of state revenue are shared across all levels of society. — Bernama

Title: Immigration DG: Actual number of refugees in Malaysia higher than UNHCR’s 200,000 figure
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, June 19 — The Immigration Department today said the actual number of refugees in Malaysia is believed to be higher than the roughly 200,000 individuals currently recorded by the United Nations High Commissioner for Refugees (UNHCR).
According to
Kosmo
, Immigration director-general Datuk Zakaria Shaaban said the government is carrying out a comprehensive registration exercise involving all refugees in the country, including those without UNHCR cards.
“We are working to register all refugees in the country, whether they have UNHCR cards or not.
“We are no longer relying solely on data supplied by UNHCR because we believe their numbers are higher than the figure given to us,” he was quoted as saying.
Zakaria said this at a press conference after the passing-out parade for the 2026 Series 1 Basic Immigration and Higher Immigration Certificate courses at the Malaysian Immigration Academy in Port Dickson today.
Also present was Home Ministry secretary-general Datuk Dr Awang Alik Jeman.
Zakaria said UNHCR records currently show more than 200,000 refugee cardholders registered in Malaysia, involving individuals from more than 140 countries.
He added that the government-coordinated registration exercise, carried out through the National Security Council, is now in its first phase and will continue until the end of this year.

Title: Pakatan top leaders will decide Puteri Wangsa seat dispute, says PKR sec-gen
Article:
JOHOR BAHRU, June 19 — PKR and Parti Amanah Negara (Amanah) leadership will hold talks soon to resolve a dispute over the Puteri Wangsa state seat ahead of the Johor state election.
PKR secretary-general Datuk Fuziah Salleh said the matter has been sent up to the top leadership of the Pakatan Harapan (PH) component parties for resolution.
“The issue has been handed over to the top leadership of the component parties to be resolved.
“The previous claim that the issue was resolved was made by Amanah, that maintained that the Puteri Wangsa seat belonged to them,” she told reporters during a Rahmah Madani programme at the Econsave Supermarket in Taman Daya here today.
Fuziah, who is also the deputy minister of domestic trade and cost of living, was commenting on the ongoing dispute between the two PH parties.
